anchors(uint32).custom:legacy: @notice Returns the anchor root. Note that this is a legacy deprecated function and will         be removed in a future release. Use getAnchorRoot() instead. Anchor roots are no         longer stored per game type, so this function will return the same root for all         game types.

params._disputeGameFinalityDelaySeconds: The dispute game finality delay in seconds.

params._disputeGameFactory: The address of the DisputeGameFactory contract.
params._systemConfig: The address of the SystemConfig contract.

returns._0: Whether the game's root claim is valid.

isGameProper(address).details: Note that isGameProper previously checked that the game type was equal to the      respected game type. However, it should be noted that it is possible for a game other      than the respected game type to resolve without being invalidated. Since isGameProper      exists to determine if a game has (or has not) been invalidated, we now allow any game      type to be considered a proper game. We enforce checks on the game type in      isGameClaimValid().

returns._0: Whether the game is a proper game.
Whether the game is of a respected game type.

isGameProper(address).notice: **READ THIS FUNCTION DOCUMENTATION CAREFULLY.**         Determines whether a game resolved properly and the game was not subject to any         invalidation conditions. The root claim of a proper game IS NOT guaranteed to be         valid. The root claim of a proper game CAN BE incorrect and still be a proper game.         DO NOT USE THIS FUNCTION ALONE TO DETERMINE IF A ROOT CLAIM IS VALID.

AnchorStateRegistry_InvalidAnchorGame().notice: Thrown when an invalid anchor game is provided.

AnchorStateRegistry_Unauthorized().notice: Thrown when an unauthorized caller attempts to set the anchor state.

ProxyAdminOwnedBase_NotProxyAdmin().notice: Thrown when the caller is not the ProxyAdmin.

ProxyAdminOwnedBase_NotProxyAdminOrProxy.notice: Thrown when the caller is not the ProxyAdmin owner or the ProxyAdmin.

ProxyAdminOwnedBase_NotProxyAdminOwner().notice: Thrown when the caller is not the ProxyAdmin owner.

ProxyAdminOwnedBase_NotResolvedDelegateP.notice: Thrown when the current contract is not a ResolvedDelegateProxy.

ProxyAdminOwnedBase_NotSharedProxyAdminO.notice: Thrown when the ProxyAdmin owner of the current contract is not the same as the         ProxyAdmin owner of the other Proxy address provided.

ProxyAdminOwnedBase_ProxyAdminNotFound().notice: Thrown when the ProxyAdmin owner of the current contract is not found.

ReinitializableBase_ZeroInitVersion().notice: Thrown when the initialization version is zero.

userdoc.notice: The AnchorStateRegistry is a contract that stores the latest "anchor" state for each available         FaultDisputeGame type. The anchor state is the latest state that has been proposed on L1 and was not         challenged within the challenge period. By using stored anchor states, new FaultDisputeGame instances can         be initialized with a more recent starting state which reduces the amount of required offchain computation.

params._disputeGameFinalityDelaySeconds: The dispute game finality delay in seconds.

params._disputeGameFactory: The address of the DisputeGameFactory contract.
params._systemConfig: The address of the SystemConfig contract.

returns._0: Whether the game's root claim is valid.

isGameProper(address).details: Note that isGameProper previously checked that the game type was equal to the      respected game type. However, it should be noted that it is possible for a game other      than the respected game type to resolve without being invalidated. Since isGameProper      exists to determine if a game has (or has not) been invalidated, we now allow any game      type to be considered a proper game. We enforce checks on the game type in      isGameClaimValid().

returns._0: Whether the game is a proper game.
Whether the game is of a respected game type.

params.game: Game that was used as the new anchor game.

params.disputeGame: The dispute game that was blacklisted.
